summary: Path-sliced VI decomp improvement touching only src/melee/vi/vi0502.c. The diff refactors un_8031E444_OnEnter from byte-indexed arg access and a reused generic gobj local toward typed ViCharaDesc field access and role-specific GObj locals, with a decomp-dev bot report showing one unmatched-item improvement for main/melee/vi/vi0502: un_8031E444_OnEnter from 98.92% to 99.66%.

key_files.role: Only changed file; updates un_8031E444_OnEnter variable typing, object-local naming, typed argument field access, and small expression/codegen-shaping details.

what_changed.text: Changed un_8031E444_OnEnter to cast arg to ViCharaDesc* desc and use desc->p1_char_index, desc->p1_costume_index, and desc->p2_costume_index instead of indexing a u8* input buffer.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: removed u8* input = arg; added ViCharaDesc* desc; desc = (ViCharaDesc*) arg;

what_changed.text: Replaced one reused HSD_GObj* gobj local with role-specific locals for fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: added HSD_GObj* fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, model_gobj

what_changed.text: Simplified erase_colors_vi0502 assignment from a GXColor pointer cast write to direct assignment from fog->color.
Adjusted model-loop codegen shape by introducing HSD_JObj* new_var as an alias for jobj and passing new_var to HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 and HSD_JObjReqAnimAll.
Changed gm_8016895C third argument from literal 0 to (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0, apparently to influence generated code while preserving a zero value.
The decomp-dev bot reported one improvement in an unmatched item for main/melee/vi/vi0502, item un_8031E444_OnEnter, from 98.92% to 99.66%.
evidence_refs: text_corpus issue_comment decomp-dev[bot]: 1 improvement in an unmatched item

smart_moves.text: Using ViCharaDesc fields instead of raw byte indices makes the entry argument contract more reviewable and aligns with typed-fields-over-pointer-math guidance.
Role-specific GObj local names clarify which created object is being configured for fog, lights, camera, and models.
Keeping the PR as a path slice under src/melee/vi reduced review scope to one file and one reported unmatched-item improvement.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Split from #2617 by directory/subsystem so the matching improvements are easier to review

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a function argument is known to be a struct-like descriptor, replacing raw byte-buffer indexing with the descriptor type and named fields can improve both readability and matching progress.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: u8* input replaced by ViCharaDesc* desc
raw_diff: input[0], input[1], input[3] replaced by desc fields

decomp_lessons.lesson: For setup functions that create several GObj instances, splitting a reused generic local into role-specific locals can clarify object lifetimes without obscuring the HSD setup sequence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gobj replaced by fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, model_gobj

naming_conventions.name: desc
naming_conventions.meaning: Local ViCharaDesc* cast from the untyped entry argument.

naming_conventions.name: char_index
naming_conventions.meaning: u8 local initialized from desc->p1_char_index and used to select the character animation archive.

naming_conventions.name: fog_gobj/light_gobj/camera_gobj/model_gobj
naming_conventions.meaning: Role-specific HSD_GObj locals for the fog, light, camera, and model setup blocks.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: each local is passed through HSD_GObjObject_80390A70 and GObj_SetupGXLink/GObj_SetupGXLinkMax or HSD_GObj_SetupProc

naming_conventions.name: new_var
naming_conventions.meaning: Temporary HSD_JObj* alias for jobj introduced in the model loop; semantic role is not explained by PR evidence.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Typed descriptor cast and field access
assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Casts arg to ViCharaDesc* and accesses named fields instead of byte offsets, likely changing/clarifying load shape while improving source fidelity.
Introduces new_var = jobj and uses the alias for selected calls in the model loop. This is supported by the diff but lacks explanatory verifier evidence beyond the bot score improvement.
Replaces literal 0 with (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0 for gm_8016895C. This appears to be a matching tactic rather than a readability improvement; no PR comment explains it.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: gm_8016895C third argument changed from 0 to (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0

assembly_or_matching_tactics.text: Replaces a pointer-cast assignment to erase_colors_vi0502 with direct assignment from fog->color.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: *(GXColor*) &erase_colors_vi0502 = fog->color; changed to erase_colors_vi0502 = fog->color;

review_feedback.text: Reported 1 improvement in an unmatched item: main/melee/vi/vi0502 un_8031E444_OnEnter, +4 bytes, 98.92% before to 99.66% after.

follow_up_queries: src/melee/vi role-specific gobj locals fog_gobj light_gobj camera_gobj model_gobj

evidence_refs: human_pr_text: PR body with scope, preliminary report, local checks
raw_diff: complete one-hunk diff for src/melee/vi/vi0502.c
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot issue comment with item-level score
notes: The changed file and diff are fully available and directly support file-specific observations.
There are no human review comments and no review records in the slice.
The PR body says per-PR CI/decomp-dev reporting is authoritative once complete; the loaded bot comment gives an item-level improvement but not a full match claim.
The body includes full unsplit branch statistics, but those are broader than this PR slice and should not be treated as this slice's exact score.
The new_var alias and expression-shaped zero are visible in the diff but lack author explanation or narrow objdiff rationale, so they should not be promoted as general style.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer recovered descriptor fields over raw byte indexing when the argument type is known
accepted_candidate_records.text: In src/melee/vi/vi0502.c, un_8031E444_OnEnter improved by replacing u8* input indexing with ViCharaDesc* desc and named fields for character and costume indices. This is a concrete example of typed field recovery improving reviewability and coinciding with a bot-reported unmatched-item improvement.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: input[0], input[1], input[3] replaced by desc fields

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use role-specific GObj local names in multi-object scene setup
accepted_candidate_records.text: This PR replaced a reused generic gobj local with fog_gobj, light_gobj, camera_gobj, and model_gobj in a VI scene setup routine, improving local role clarity while preserving the setup sequence.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: role-specific HSD_GObj* locals and uses

source_update_candidates.title: src/melee/vi/vi0502.c contains VI scene setup entry un_8031E444_OnEnter using ViCharaDesc
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2630 evidence shows src/melee/vi/vi0502.c includes un_8031E444_OnEnter, which casts its void* argument to ViCharaDesc* and uses p1_char_index, p1_costume_index, and p2_costume_index while loading Vi0502.dat, visual0502Scene, character animation data, fog, lights, camera, and models.
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful path-local fact for future VI work; should be verified against current source before adding to path facts.

rejection_notes: Do not promote new_var as a naming convention; PR evidence only shows it as a temporary alias with no semantic explanation.
Do not promote (un_804D6F90->models[i] != NULL) * 0 as accepted style; it appears to be a narrow matching tactic and conflicts with the need for explicit evidence for codegen tactics.
Do not treat the unsplit branch's +11944 matched-code bytes or 26 new matches as this PR slice's exact result; the loaded slice bot report only names one unmatched-item improvement.
